2. [reddit, subreddits] User's Reddit scanner focuses on subreddits: sociology, philosophy, technology, programming, MachineLearning, EffectiveAltruism, DepthHub, skeptic, ec...
3. [reddit, cron] User set up a cron job to run the Reddit scanner every hour
4. [tech, reddit, filter] User's Reddit scanner filters for posts with 300+ upvotes and 10+ comments
5. [reddit, subreddits] User scans subreddits: sociology, philosophy, technology, programming, MachineLearning, EffectiveAltruism, DepthHub, skeptic, economics, science
6. [reddit, filter] User filters Reddit posts requiring 300+ upvotes and 10+ comments
7. [reddit, filter] User excludes political CMV posts and lifestyle/dating/personal posts from Reddit scans
8. [reddit, notification] User sends new quality Reddit findings via PocketClaw
9. [tech, script, reddit] User has a Reddit scanner script located at /home/xionghouyuan2/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/reddit-scanner.py
10. [tech, cron, reddit] User set up a cron job named 'Reddit Quality Scanner' to run hourly at Asia/Shanghai timezone
</relevant-memories>

[cron:bee81945-c0fc-4c48-aeb6-71403149938f Reddit Quality Scanner] Run the Reddit scanner script to find quality content and send a report to the user.

Execute this command:
```bash
python3 /home/xionghouyuan2/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/reddit-scanner.py
```

The script will:
1. Scan multiple subreddits for high-quality posts (excluding politics, lifestyle trivial posts, posts under 300 upvotes)
2. Filter out already-seen posts using a cache
3. Send any new quality findings to the user via PocketClaw

If no new quality posts are found, do not send a report.

Quality criteria:
- Posts must have 300+ upvotes and 10+ comments
- Exclude CMV political posts
- Exclude lifestyle/dating/personal posts
- Focus on: sociology, philosophy, technology, programming, AI/ML, economics, science
Current time: Monday, April 6th, 2026 — 8:02 PM (Asia/Shanghai) / 2026-04-06 12:02 UTC

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.
